So Instagram's servers literally exploded when 170 million refugees from the TikTok apocalypse showed up at once, because apparently Meta's infrastructure team never heard of load balancing. But sure, let's trust these people with the metaverse.

Chinese tech stocks nosedived 15% faster than you can say 'digital cold war' as Wall Street realized maybe putting all our eggs in the global connectivity basket wasn't genius. Who could have predicted that geopolitics might affect our precious algorithms?

The EU suddenly discovered they have a spine and fast-tracked digital sovereignty laws, because nothing says 'we're independent' like waiting for America to do something first. Brussels bureaucrats are probably high-fiving themselves for this groundbreaking original thinking.

YouTube ad rates shot up 40% as desperate brands realized they need somewhere to hawk their products now that dance videos are politically complicated. Marketing executives are probably crying into their kombucha about having to actually pay fair prices for attention.

Influencer marketing agencies watched 60% of their revenue evaporate as the creator economy shattered into a million pieces, leaving thousands of lifestyle coaches with nothing but ring lights and existential dread. Thoughts and prayers for the personal brand consultants.

Food delivery apps buckled under the weight of displaced food reviewers who apparently think DoorDash is the new frontier of culinary criticism. These platforms went from delivering mediocre pad thai to being the backbone of gastronomic journalism, somehow.

A LOCAL BURRITO SHOP had to raise prices because of DELAYED AVOCADO SHIPMENTS and food bloggers, and honestly this is where I draw the line because you DO NOT mess with affordable Mexican food over some supply chain nonsense and wannabe Yelp reviewers who think adding cilantro is a personality trait!
